que: In Test Coverage, what is the primary goal?
options: measure how much code tests execute
replace testing entirely
avoid understanding requirements
skip state/data modeling

que: Which API/concept is most central to Test Coverage?
options: random utility class name
manual polling loop for all tasks
coverage report
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic

que: What is a recommended best practice for Test Coverage?
options: ignore edge/error states
optimize before verifying correctness
mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
focus on meaningful behavior coverage

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing Test Coverage?
options: keep boundaries clear
chasing 100% without quality assertions
follow official docs for API usage
write focused tests for behavior

que: For production code, which approach fits Test Coverage best?
options: Use Test Coverage while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use Test Coverage with clear state flow and testable logic
Use Test Coverage and suppress all warnings
Use Test Coverage only as theory with no implementation

que: A teammate asks where to start with Test Coverage. What is best advice?
options: avoid feedback from tests/review
Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/studio/test/test-in-android-studio#view-test-coverage) and implement a small sample
copy an unknown snippet without context
skip running the app after changes

que: Which statement about Test Coverage is most accurate?
options: Test Coverage removes the need for any testing
Test Coverage is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
Test Coverage should be applied identically in every layer
Test Coverage is mainly about adding boilerplate

que: In a code review for Test Coverage, what would you flag?
options: Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly
explicit modeling of UI/data states
traceable data flow between layers
small pure functions with clear names

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Test Coverage correctly?
options: a slide deck with no runnable code
a large refactor without tests
an implementation with no error states
A small, working feature that demonstrates coverage report in context

que: To learn Test Coverage deeply, what should you do next?
options: memorize definitions only
skip verification to save time
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Test Coverage patterns
avoid official references
